title: will / shall
examples: If you have any other K drama recommendations please let me know in the comments because I'll be watching a lot more K dramas from here on out.

So I think it will be a good chance to focus on some of my personal goals, like continuing to try and exercise more regularly, getting on a better sleep schedule, practicing my Korean, these kinds of things.
Thank you for watching and I will see you next time.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: When I have free time and I'm not watching a K drama I've been reading books which is pretty normal for me. I love to read books.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I'm not really a big gym rat and I think doing workouts at home can be more comfortable and convenient.
Maybe it's because I can relate to them.
So they can be a bit confusing but always towards the end of her books I get hooked and I cannot put them down.
The tent actually floats on water and you can fish right off of the deck of your tent.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: You must be very familiar with her now.

title: should (advice)
examples: In order to change that habit I told myself if I want to watch TV I should watch a Korean drama instead.
This upcoming week should be a little bit more chill.

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If I was washing the dishes I also watched a show. While I was folding laundry I also watched a show.
If you have any other K drama recommendations please let me know in the comments because I'll be watching a lot more K dramas from here on out.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: I was invited to a skincare event by Skinchansa.

title: have something done
examples: I love lead female characters that have a little bit of an attitude.
